name: Monstera tacanaensis
origin: Mexico (Chiapas) to Guatemala, Costa Rica and Panama
aboutText: Monstera tacanaensis is a captivating lianescent climber native to the tropical forests ranging from Mexico through to Panama. Closely related to the iconic Monstera deliciosa, it is distinguished by its more segmented foliage which features numerous slits and elongated perforations extending toward the midrib. Thriving in high-rainfall, shaded understories, this species utilizes robust petioles with strong geniculums to ascend host trees in its natural habitat. It is a highly sought-after specimen for collectors who appreciate its intricate leaf architecture and elegant, airy appearance compared to its larger cousins.

fieldNotes.title: Tacaná Volcano Origins
fieldNotes.author: Aroid Aaron
fieldNotes.content: Named for the Tacaná volcano on the Mexico-Guatemala border — the cone that marks the transition between Mexican Chiapas and the Guatemalan highlands. A climbing Monstera distributed from this origin through Central America and Panama, in humid cloud forest conditions. At middle tier pricing. The geological specificity of the name is pleasantly rare in plant taxonomy — most species are named for people or descriptive qualities, and the volcano origin gives this one a biogeographic context that is genuinely informative.
